title: Tephroseris pricei
intro: Tephroseris pricei, also known as Price's Mountain Everlasting, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris pricei include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Tephroseris pricei is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to China, specifically in the provinces of Yunnan and Sichuan. The classification of Tephroseris pricei is further divided into the genus Tephroseris, which is a group of plants formerly classified as a part of the genus Seriphium. The genus Tephroseris is part of the tribe Senecioneae, which also includes other daisy-like plants. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the broader plant kingdom.
